subject: 📊 Daily Portfolio: +1.4% today | NVDA stop tested intraday, AVGO recovering
html_body:
Daily Portfolio Check

Friday, June 12, 2026 — Kuwait Time (AST)

Total P&L (vs cost basis)

−$0.43 (-0.07%)

Today's Change

+$8.50 (+1.38%)

🎯 Withdrawal Goal

$828.33 / $1,000 — $171.67 to go (82.8%)

NVDA tested its $204 stop intraday (low $201.61) but held on close; AVGO recovering +3.6% from post-earnings lows — stop remains only 1.73% away. Both need Monday morning attention.

🚨 Decisions Needed This Weekend

NVDA: Stop $204 tested intraday today (low $201.61). If it opens below $204 Monday, exit is warranted. Decide pre-market.
AVGO: Stop $379 is 1.73% below Friday close of $385.57. Data suggests possible close below stop on June 11. Verify your execution — if you use closing stops, this may already be triggered.
LLY stop update: Stored stop $915 sits below 200-day MA ($960). Consider updating to $960 for technical validity.

Position Snapshot

StatusTickerCloseDayP&L $P&L %
⚠️ WATCHNVDA$205.96+0.53%−$15.85−5.28%
✅ HOLDLLY$1,161.82 *N/A †+$16.43+16.75%
⚠️ WATCHAVGO$385.57+3.62%−$8.34−6.42%
✅ HOLDTSM$421.07+3.01%+$7.33+8.42%
TOTAL (positions)—+1.38%−$0.43−0.07%

* LLY: June 12 close unconfirmed — using June 11 data. † Day change not calculated for LLY.

Position Detail

NVDA NVIDIA Corporation ⚠️ WATCH$205.96 +0.53%

Stop tested intraday. Today's low was $201.61 — that's $2.39 below the stored stop of $204. Closed at $205.96, above stop on a closing basis. Real support: 50-day MA $203.22. The intraday breach is a warning shot — if Monday opens below $203, stop should be executed. Thesis intact (AI demand strong, Q1 FY27 revenue +85% YoY, analyst consensus $298). 1.3799 × $205.96 = $284.21 current value.

Stop: $200–201 (below 50-day MA $203 + today's intraday low; stored $204 now needs tightening)
TP1: $219 (prior resistance zone) | TP2: $229–232 (pivot R1) | TP3: $236.54 (52-wk high)

LLY Eli Lilly ✅ HOLD$1,161.82 *Jun 11

Foundayo (orforglipron) FDA-approved April 1 — now in weeks 8–12 of US commercial launch, the key ramp window analysts are watching. June 8 ADA data showed superiority over oral semaglutide head-to-head. GLP-1 duopoly intact. 0.0986 × $1,161.82 = $114.54 current value.

Stop: $960 (just below 200-day MA $960.23; stored $915 has no technical level — recommend updating via portfolio-manager)
TP1: $1,108 (prior resistance) | TP2: $1,170 (Jun 11 high) | TP3: ~$1,250 (analyst consensus range)

AVGO Broadcom Inc. ⚠️ WATCH$385.57 +3.62%

Post-earnings breakdown under repair. Q2 FY26 was strong (AI revenue +143% YoY, total +48%) but Q3 AI guidance of $16B missed buyside whisper of $17.2B — stock fell ~13.78% after-hours June 3. Macquarie downgraded June 4 on Google insourcing risk (custom ASIC threat). Estimated June 11 close ~$372 suggests possible close below stored stop $379. Today recovered +3.62% to $385.57. Stop is 1.73% away. 0.315 × $385.57 = $121.45 current value.

⚠️ Data suggests AVGO may have closed below stop $379 on June 11 (~$372 estimated). If you use closing stops, this may already be a triggered exit. Verify your execution methodology before Monday open.

Stop: $365–367 (below post-earnings low; stored $379 violated — update needed)
TP1: $412 (entry / prior support now resistance) | TP2: $429 (20-day MA zone)
Exit math if stop $379 triggers: 0.315 × ($379 − $412.04) = −$10.41 gross | −$3 commission = −$13.41 net loss

TSM Taiwan Semiconductor ✅ HOLD$421.07 +3.01%

Recovering strongly with the semi sector. AI foundry demand is the strongest macro tailwind in the portfolio. 0.224 × $421.07 = $94.32. Stop well below. TP1 at $435 is only 3.3% away — watch for a trim opportunity as it approaches.

Stop: $398–402 (support cluster; stored $357 is too loose — consider updating)
TP1: $427–435 ⚡ 3.3% away | TP2: $452–453 (R1 resistance cluster)

🔍 Risk Scores

NVDA | 42/100 | MODERATE | 48.8% concentration + stop proximity + intraday breach today
LLY | 17/100 | LOW | GLP-1 duopoly intact; Foundayo launch ramp weeks 8–12
AVGO | 55/100 | HIGH | Post-earnings break; Google insourcing risk; stop 1.73% away
TSM | 26/100 | LOW | Strong AI foundry demand; geopolitical tail risk binary

⏳ Pending Watchlist — Waiting on IBKR Approval

VRDN Viridian TherapeuticsWATCH 6/10

🔒 IBKR application pending — current broker does not allow this stock

Live: $17.63  |  Entry target: $20.00  ⚠️ Dropped 11.9% below target — verify thesis still intact

Stop: $14.50 | TP1: $28.00 | TP2: $37.00

🟢 Veligrotug PDUFA (Thyroid Eye Disease) — June 30, 2026 | 18 days away

Approval probability: 82%

Halal: ⚠️ UNVERIFIED — check Musaffa (interest income vs milestone revenue ratio unclear)

Price drop to $17.63 from $20 target (−11.9%) needs investigation — verify the veligrotug PDUFA thesis is independent of REVEAL-1 results. IBKR approval needed before entry. When approved: consider entering at current ~$17.63 (better than $20 target), stop $14.50.

VERA Vera TherapeuticsBUY NOW 7/10

🔒 IBKR application pending — current broker does not allow this stock

Live: ~$31.60  |  Entry target: $34.04  ✅ Entry valid (7.2% below target — within range)

Stop: $24.50 | TP1: $55.00 (50%) | TP2: $78.00 (50%)

🟢 Atacicept PDUFA (IgA Nephropathy) — July 7, 2026 | 25 days away

Approval probability: 89%

Halal: ⚠️ UNVERIFIED — check Musaffa (pre-revenue biotech, term debt structure)

BTD + Priority Review + p<0.0001 Phase 3 + $5.28M director open-market buy. Zero CRL precedent in IgAN indication. Window narrowing fast.

⚠️ Only 25 days to PDUFA. If IBKR not approved in time, this entry window closes before the catalyst.

📡 Catalyst Plays — June 12, 2026

Live-scanned from FDA calendar and biotech trackers. Sorted by urgency. All halal unverified — check Musaffa before entering any.

SPRO Spero TherapeuticsWATCH 2/5

Catalyst: Tebipenem HBr NDA (complicated UTI) — PDUFA June 18 — 6 DAYS 🔴 URGENT

Market cap $143M | Price ~$2.48 | GSK-partnered oral carbapenem antibiotic

Approval prob: ~75% (Phase 3 PIVOT-PO met primary endpoint, stopped early for efficacy)

✅ If approved: ~$5–8 (+100–220%) | 💀 If rejected: ~$1–1.50 (−40–60%)

Smart money: GSK partnership validates science. No insider buys. Halal ⚠️ unverified. Too small ($143M) + halal gap = WATCH not INVESTIGATE. Say 'analyze SPRO' for full deep dive.

ACHV Achieve Life SciencesWATCH 3/5

Catalyst: Cytisinicline NDA (smoking cessation) — PDUFA June 20 — 8 DAYS 🟡 SOON

Market cap $572M | Price ~$5.57 | Plant-derived nicotine replacement, non-addictive

Approval prob: ~55% (smoking cessation base rate; FDA National Priority Voucher received = regulatory tailwind; but therapeutic area has mixed FDA history)

✅ If approved: ~$10–14 (+80–150%) | 💀 If rejected: ~$2.50–3.50 (−37–55%)

Smart money: FDA Priority Voucher = positive regulatory signal. No classic insider buys. Halal ⚠️ unverified.

Highest conviction of today's three catalyst plays. Check Musaffa first. Say 'analyze ACHV' for full deep dive.

ARQT Arcutis BiotherapeuticsSKIP 2/5

Catalyst: Zoryve sNDA (pediatric psoriasis ages 2–5) — PDUFA June 29 — 17 days 🟢

Revenue-generating ($105M Q1, cash-flow positive). Indication expansion only — lower binary risk and lower upside. Score 2/5. Not compelling vs. current portfolio priorities. Shown for transparency.

💵 Cash Deployment — $213.81 available

Reserve $6 → Deployable: $207.81. After $3 commission → $204.81 net into position.

Recommended: Hold as dry powder through next week.

NVDA and AVGO are both within striking distance of their stops. If either triggers, freed capital ($284 NVDA or $122 AVGO) becomes available. Deploying $204 into a new position while two existing positions face potential stop-outs clusters your action at the worst possible moment. Wait for the NVDA/AVGO situation to resolve first — then redeploy with conviction.

If you want to deploy now anyway (two options):

Option A — ACHV catalyst play: 36 shares × $5.57 = $200.52 + $3 commission = $203.52 total. PDUFA June 20 (8 days). Upside +80–150%, downside −37–55%. Halal ⚠️ unverified — check Musaffa first. 30% portfolio cap: $248 max → $200 fits.
Option B — Add to TSM (26/100, LOW risk): 0.48 shares × ~$421 = $202.08 + $3 = $205.08 total. Blended avg falls to ~$397. New TSM value ~$296 (35.7% of portfolio). Conviction play on AI foundry structural demand.
Your call.

🔄 Rotation Suggestions

No rotations suggested today. Rotation rules require trimming a winner, not a loser — AVGO is down 6.42%, making it ineligible as a rotation source. LLY (+16.75%) could theoretically be trimmed, but no INVESTIGATE-level catalyst play justifies it, and the LLY thesis is strengthening with Foundayo's launch ramp. Best move: no move. Let the stops do their job. Review next week with a fresh catalyst scan.

What to do this weekend / Monday

1. AVGO: Decide closing vs intraday stop methodology. Verify June 11 close. Set hard alert at $379 for Monday pre-market.
2. NVDA: Set Monday pre-market alert at $203 (50-day MA). Open below $203 = execute stop.
3. LLY stop: Update $915 → $960 (200-day MA) via portfolio-manager.
4. VRDN: Check if price drop to $17.63 changes entry thesis before June 30 PDUFA.
5. VERA: Push IBKR application — 25 days to PDUFA, every day costs you entry flexibility.

📚 Today you learned — Concept #16

Earnings Whisper Numbers

Your AVGO position just demonstrated this concept live. On June 3, Broadcom guided Q3 AI revenue at $16 billion — technically matching the sell-side consensus. The stock dropped 13.78% after hours anyway. The reason: the buy-side — the hedge funds and institutions who actually move markets — had a private estimate of $17.2 billion in their models. That number is the whisper. It never appears on Yahoo Finance. When the print hits $16B and the whisper was $17.2B, retail traders read "beat" and buy; institutional traders read "miss" and sell. Price goes down on a consensus beat because the whisper is what actually matters. Before any of your holdings report earnings, always ask not what consensus expects, but how high the buy-side bar actually is. For NVDA's next print, consensus EPS might be $0.90 — if the whisper is $1.05, a $0.95 print still trades as a miss.

Also today: Beat-and-raise — beating current-quarter estimates matters far less than raising forward guidance; the guidance raise sets the next whisper cycle.

Research output, not financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results. Verify halal compliance quarterly on Zoya or Musaffa. Prices as of June 12, 2026 (NVDA, AVGO, TSM confirmed; LLY using June 11 close — unconfirmed for June 12). Sources: Yahoo Finance, CNBC, Seeking Alpha, StockAnalysis, MarketBeat, StockTitan, Barchart, Investing.com, BioPharmWatch, BioPharmCatalyst. Generated June 12, 2026 — Kuwait Time (AST).
